quality.rationale: 结合正交标架定义与外微分运算证明超曲面正交条件，需多步推理且具明确黑洞吸积盘物理背景

origin.question:
[文件 1: 1_s17_17.18_c.md]

Problem 17.18 (c) The “Zero Angular Momentum Observers” (ZAMO's) in the Kerr geometry have basis 1-forms
$$
\begin{array}{l} \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {t}} = \left| g_{t t} - \omega^{2} g_{\phi \phi} \right|^{\frac {1}{2}} \widetilde {d t} \\
\tilde {\omega}^{\hat {\phi}} = \left(\mathrm {g}_{\phi \phi}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} \left(\widetilde {\mathrm {d} \phi} - \omega \widetilde {\mathrm {d t}}\right) \\
\tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\mathbf {r}}} = \left(\boldsymbol {\Sigma} / \Delta\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} \overline {{\mathrm {d} \mathbf {r}}} \\
\tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\theta}} = \Sigma^{\frac {1}{2}} \widetilde {\mathbf {d} \theta} \\
\end{array}
$$
where $\omega \equiv -\mathbf{g}_{t\phi} / \mathbf{g}_{\phi \phi}$
(c) The 4-velocity of the ZAMO is $\mathbf{u} = \mathbf{e}_{\hat{\mathbf{t}}}$ ; show that $\mathbf{u}$ has zero rotation.
origin.answer: $\\mathbf{d}\\widetilde{\\boldsymbol{\\omega}}^{\\mathrm{t}}\\wedge \\widetilde{\\boldsymbol{\\omega}}^{\\mathrm{t}} = 0$，即四速度场满足超曲面正交条件，具有零旋转
origin.solution:
(c) The 4-velocity has zero rotation if $\omega_{\alpha}\beta = 0$ (see Problem 5.18) or equivalently if $\mathbf{u}$ is hypersurface orthogonal:
$$
\mathrm {u}_{[ \alpha ; \beta} \mathrm {u}_{\gamma ]} = 0
$$
(see Problem 7.23). The quickest way to check this condition is to use forms and the equivalence of $\mathfrak{u}[\hat{\alpha};\hat{\beta}]$ and $\mathbf{d}\tilde{\boldsymbol{\omega}}^{\mathrm{t}}$ . The condition $\mathfrak{u}[\hat{\alpha};\hat{\beta}\mathbf{u}_{\hat{\gamma}}] = 0$ is then equivalent to $\mathbf{d}\tilde{\boldsymbol{\omega}}^{\mathrm{t}}\wedge \tilde{\boldsymbol{\omega}}^{\mathrm{t}} = 0$ . But $	ilde{\boldsymbol{\omega}}^{\mathrm{t}} = \boldsymbol{a}\mathrm{d}t$ where $a = |\mathbf{g}_{\mathrm{tt}} - \omega^{2}\mathbf{g}_{\phi \phi}|^{\frac{1}{2}} = a(\mathbf{r},\theta)$ and thus
$$
\mathrm {d} \tilde {\omega}^{\mathrm {t}} = a,_{\mathrm {r}} \widetilde {\mathrm {d r}} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d} t} + a,_{\theta} \widetilde {\mathrm {d} \theta} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d} t}
$$
so $\mathbf{d}\widetilde{\boldsymbol{\omega}}^{\mathrm{t}}\wedge \widetilde{\boldsymbol{\omega}}^{\mathrm{t}} = 0$ since $\widetilde{\mathrm{dt}}\wedge \widetilde{\mathrm{dt}} = 0$
